item.title: China Holds U.S. Seismologist on Espionage Charges
item.description: Youlin Chen’s research focused on nuclear testing by North Korea.

item.title: Forget France: China Is the New Foie Gras Champion
item.description: The Asian country makes 11,000 tons of the bird-liver delicacy annually—and it costs a little more than half the French kind.

item.title: Deadly Factory Fire Highlights Dangers of China’s Manufacturing Might
item.description: The blaze, which killed 28 people, comes shortly after similar incidents at a coal mine and another at a fireworks factory.

item.title: He Called Out China’s Economy. Then He Went Silent—for Good.
item.description: Plus, Beijing’s AI ambitions and why some readers don’t trust Chinese EVs

item.title: How Xi Went From Admiring Putin to Managing Him
item.description: Plus, data-center builders are racing to offload stakes worth billions, and the superrich are snapping up neighboring properties to create private compounds.

item.title: China Exports Surged in June, Exceeding Market Expectations
item.description: China’s exports defied forecasts of a slowdown, providing a vital boost to an economy still weighed down by weak domestic demand.

item.title: China Successfully Launches Reusable Rocket in a Win for Space Program
item.description: China launched and partially recovered a rocket, a milestone for Beijing’s space program as it competes with the U.S. and Elon Musk’s SpaceX.

item.title: China’s Factory-Price Growth Quickens, Consumer Inflation Eases
item.description: China’s factory-gate inflation accelerated in June as the effects of the Middle East conflict continued to ripple through the economy, but consumer-price gains cooled, suggesting persistent weakness in domestic demand.

item.title: As China Encroaches, Even New Zealand Is Getting Serious About Its Military
item.description: Billions will be spent in the coming years—including on missiles, drones and base upgrades—as the remote country squares up to a widening threat.

item.title: The U.S. Navy’s New Insurance Policy for War With China Is an Australian Base
item.description: The Pentagon plans to deploy submarines to an expanding facility in allies’ strategy to deter Beijing.
